results.title: Singapore's Sea tests AI chatbot Migoo in quiet US foray - The Business Times
results.text:
Singapore's Sea tests AI chatbot Migoo in quiet US foray - The Business Times

# Singapore’s Sea tests AI chatbot Migoo in quiet US foray

The project represents one of the company’s most significant steps into AI thus far

Summarise

Share

Add BT as a preferred source

Add BT as a preferred source

Published Wed, Jun 17, 2026 · 05:41 PM

In the US, Sea is going after the lucrative and very competitive Gen-Z AI market. PHOTO: REUTERS

[SINGAPORE] Sea is rolling out a generative-AI chatbot in regions including the US, marking its maiden foray into the highly competitive American consumer market and accelerating its artificial intelligence push.

The AI-powered companion chatbot called Migoo, whose website and app doesn’t mention Sea’s ownership, is already online and may get a bigger launch soon, according to people familiar with the matter.

The app integrates directly into platforms like Apple’s iMessage and is designed to act as a hyper-personalised companion that remembers user preferences and quirks.

The project represents one of Sea’s most significant steps into AI thus far. The operator of popular online retailer Shopee and gaming platform Garena has seen its shares decline more than 40 per cent over the past year as it has trailed Asian peers such as Alibaba Group Holding and Tencent Holdings in the field.

In the US, Sea is going after the lucrative and very competitive Gen-Z AI market dominated by the likes of OpenAI and ByteDance, the people said, asking not to be named discussing private plans.

A Sea spokesperson declined to comment.

### Asean Intelligence

Get insights into businesses across South-east Asia

Get the free report

https://www.businesstimes.com.sg/keywords/asean-intelligence-2026?ref=asean-intelligence-article-banner

Sea is making structural shifts after chief executive officer Forrest Li declared that a trillion-US dollar market capitalisation was possible if his company doubled down on AI. It joins a growing number of companies like rival Alibaba that are investing in AI to catalyse growth while competition in their core businesses intensifies.

Migoo is spearheaded by Sea president Chris Feng, and managers involved include longtime executive Bingyu Wang, the people said. Migoo’s website is up and running and the app is downloadable, but Sea has yet to publicly claim ownership of the project.

In the US, Migoo operates under Marvelous Technology, an entity registered to a known proxy address in Sacramento, according to California’s company registry. Filings show that the US unit is linked to a Singapore entity that lists Wang as a director.

SEE ALSO

### Shopee cuts hundreds of developer jobs globally during pivot to AI

Shopee cuts hundreds of developer jobs globally during pivot to AI

### Singapore’s Sea sets up AI investment team as part of pivot beyond e-commerce

Singapore’s Sea sets up AI investment team as part of pivot beyond e-commerce

### Grab, Sea lean on affordability, subscriptions to defend growth amid macroeconomic headwinds

Grab, Sea lean on affordability, subscriptions to defend growth amid macroeconomic headwinds

### Sea reports 6.2% increase in Q1 earnings to US$427.9 million

Sea reports 6.2% increase in Q1 earnings to US$427.9 million

### Sea to launch new AI centre to drive innovation and capability-building in Singapore

Sea to launch new AI centre to drive innovation and capability-building in Singapore

### Sea shares slump 16.5%; analysts deem drop an ‘overreaction’

Sea shares slump 16.5%; analysts deem drop an ‘overreaction’

More

Sea’s strategy with Migoo resembles the playbook ByteDance used to launch its viral AI homework app Gauth and Alibaba to introduce its Happy Oyster model. By not publicising the ultimate corporate parent from the start, tech companies are aiming to test the waters for more new-fangled initiatives, affording these projects more freedom to experiment.

Sea’s stock has taken a beating since September – when it was valued at roughly US$116 billion – after this year’s surge in oil prices dampened consumer sentiment and inflated the cost of operations. Investors are now scrutinising its growth trajectory.

So far, Sea has been embedding AI in small ways like through product recommendations and seller tools.

In February, it said that it would work with Alphabet’s Google to integrate AI across its operations – including developing AI shopping agents. It has also set up dedicated teams to scout for new investments in AI as it hunts for its next growth engine beyond e-commerce.

Meanwhile, its online-retail arm Shopee is cutting hundreds of developer jobs globally, or about 8 per cent of the unit’s developer workforce. It is unclear if the move is directly tied to Sea’s forays into AI, but they coincide with a growing debate about the impact of the technology on jobs across the world. BLOOMBERG

results.title: Singapore-based Sea Ltd seeks to expand to banking, financial services in Vietnam - TNGlobal
results.author: Duc Dao
results.text:
Singapore-based Sea Ltd seeks to expand to banking, financial services in Vietnam - TNGlobal

## Singapore-based Sea Ltd seeks to expand to banking, financial services in Vietnam

June 1, 2026• eCommerce, News, Vietnam•

Singapore-headquartered e-commerce and gaming giant Sea Ltd, known for e-commerce platform Shopee, plans to expand its operations in Vietnam into banking, financial services, and modern logistics,

Forrest Li, founder, chairman, and CEO of Sea Ltd made the statement at a meeting with Vietnamese General Secretary-President To Lam last Saturday, on the sidelines of the latter’s state visit to Singapore.

For his part, To Lam agreed with the proposal to deepen cooperation and encouraged Sea Limited to enlarge activities in Vietnam, such as e-commerce growth, support for small businesses with digital transformation and sales channels, and digital supply chains integration.

The country’s top leader also called on Sea Limited to explore expanded cooperation in artificial intelligence (AI), data, and digital technology to help small businesses, household enterprises, and Vietnamese sellers improve their operational capabilities and reach new markets.

He also encouraged the group to promote cross-border e-commerce, develop digital payments and digital financial services, and advance inclusive financial solutions in Vietnam.

According to Vietnam’s e-commerce data analyzer Metric.vn, Shopee was the biggest e-commerce platform in Vietnam as of end-2025 with a market share of 56 percent, down from 64 percent in 2024. The runner-up was TikTok Shop with 41 percent, up from 29 percent in 2024. Together, Shopee and TikTok Shop accounted for nearly 100 percent of the e-commerce market in Vietnam.

Singapore’s Sea Ltd partners with OpenAI on new ways to shop

results.title: Singapore's Sea sets up AI investment team as part of pivot beyond e-commerce - The Business Times
results.text:
Singapore's Sea sets up AI investment team as part of pivot beyond e-commerce - The Business Times

# Singapore’s Sea sets up AI investment team as part of pivot beyond e-commerce

Sea has been embedding AI in small ways, like through product recommendations and seller tools

Share

Add BT as a preferred source

Add BT as a preferred source

Published Fri, May 29, 2026 · 01:40 PM — Updated Fri, May 29, 2026 · 08:19 PM

Sea, the operator of online retailer Shopee, is making structural shifts after CEO Forrest Li declared last year that a trillion-dollar market capitalisation was possible if the company doubled down on AI and “makes the right calls”. PHOTO: BT FILE

SEA has set up a dedicated team to scout for new investments in artificial intelligence, part of a broader effort to accelerate forays into the technology as it hunts for its next growth engine beyond e-commerce.

The investment unit sits directly under the president’s office, and is one of several initiatives Sea has established to dedicate capital towards internal and external AI projects, according to people familiar with the matter.

The group, led by long-time executive Zhang Endong, evaluates potential investments in startups globally, said the people, who asked not to be identified discussing private information.

Zhang also spearheads several other recently established projects dedicated to analysing AI adoption within the company itself, the people added.

His startup investment team is one of several groups within Sea’s sprawling organisation that are exploring similar opportunities.

Zhang is one of the key executives tasked with helping to steer a company-wide effort to adopt and deploy AI.

### Asean Intelligence

Get insights into businesses across South-east Asia

Get the free report

Sea, the operator of online retailer Shopee and the Garena gaming platform, is making structural shifts after CEO Forrest Li declared last year that a trillion-dollar market capitalisation was possible if the company doubled down on AI and “makes the right calls”.

It joins a growing number of companies, like rival Alibaba Group Holding, that are investing in AI to catalyse growth while competition in their core business intensifies.

A company spokesperson declined to comment on that endeavour.

SEE ALSO

### Sea to launch new AI centre to drive innovation and capability-building in Singapore

### Sea reports 6.2% increase in Q1 earnings to US$427.9 million

### Grab, Sea lean on affordability, subscriptions to defend growth amid macroeconomic headwinds

### Sea and Google ink partnership to develop AI-powered tools

More

The company’s stock has taken a beating since September – when it was valued at roughly US$116 billion – after this year’s surge in oil prices dampened consumer sentiment and inflated the cost of operations. Investors are now scrutinising its growth trajectory.

So far, Sea has been embedding AI in small ways, like through product recommendations and seller tools. In February, it said it would work with Alphabet’s Google to integrate AI across its operations – including developing AI shopping agents – to stay ahead of rivals.

South-east Asia’s dominant e-commerce player joins a wave of online retailers including Amazon.com and Japan’s Rakuten Group racing to roll out AI-powered shopping services.

At home, it is facing off against Alibaba’s Lazada, which last year launched AI agents that help with refunds, delivery and marketing.

That is while ByteDance’s TikTok beefs up its AI tools with functions like helping merchants convert still images into videos with voiceovers.

The Chinese company is discussing expenditures of as much as US$70 billion this year as it builds out data centres and other AI infrastructure, according to people familiar with the matter. BLOOMBERG

results.title: Sea establishes AI Center of Excellence in Singapore to drive AI innovation, capability building - TNGlobal
results.author: TechNode Global Staff
results.text:
Sea establishes AI Center of Excellence in Singapore to drive AI innovation, capability building - TNGlobal

## Sea establishes AI Center of Excellence in Singapore to drive AI innovation, capability building

April 21, 2026• AI, News, Singapore•

Singapore-based technology firm Sea Limited announced Monday the establishment of an Artificial Intelligence Center of Excellence (AI CoE) in the country, with support from Digital Industry Singapore (DISG), a joint office by Singapore Economic Development Board, Enterprise Singapore, and Infocomm Media Development Authority.

The AI CoE reinforces Sea’s continued commitment to investing in its proprietary technology stack and advances its ambitions towards becoming an AI-native company, the firm said in a statement.

Organized around Sea’s technology-first approach to innovation, the AI CoE will support continuous breakthroughs in both the research and business application of AI, as well as contribute towards strategic capability building in the following areas: foundational AI, scalable deployment, AI-native talent and new operating models.

Foundational AI is an advance foundation model development, evaluation frameworks, and internal tooling to stay close to frontier developments in the field.

The center also translate research and development (R&D) advances into production-ready solutions that can be sustainably deployed at-scale to enhance user experience, efficiency, and other measurable business objectives.

It also nurtures a pool of AI-native talent and experiment with new operating models enabled by AI technology.

Over the next three years, the AI CoE is expected to create demand for at least 100 R&D and innovation-centric roles in Singapore, with activities spanning AI research, engineering, and product development.

Through disciplined investment into cutting-edge R&D, the AI CoE will also deepen the sophistication and application of Sea’s growing stable of in-house AI models.

This includes self-developed foundation models such as Compass Max v3.5, a 245 billion parameter Large Language Model (LLM) tailored for Southeast Asian languages and e-commerce contexts.

Compass Max v3.5 and its variants are already in live use powering numerous AI features across Shopee’s e-commerce platform – operating with greater responsiveness and at a fraction of the cost of other leading commercially served LLMs.

“As AI continues to evolve, we see it as a foundational capability that strengthens how we build innovative products, operate at a global scale, and create value for the countless communities we serve,” said Forrest Li, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, Sea.

“This AI Centre of Excellence reflects Sea’s long-term commitment to investing in talent and innovation in Singapore,

“Such homegrown AI capabilities also contribute to the wider AI ecosystem in Singapore, which we are excited to partner with the government on growing and developing,” he added.

Sea’s AI CoE will deepen homegrown AI capabilities, accelerate enterprise adoption of AI, and develop an AI-ready talent pool. This initiative, supported by DISG, aligns with Singapore’s National AI Strategy 2.0 and Budget 2026 priorities.

“This investment by Sea strengthens our position as a global AI hub with cutting-edge capabilities in Singapore. The new AI CoE will create new innovation roles in areas such as AI engineering and product development, providing Singaporeans an opportunity to create AI products with a global reach,

“We look forward to the cutting-edge work that will emerge from Sea’s Center of Excellence in Singapore.” said Philbert Gomez, Senior Vice President, Executive Director and Head, Digital Industry Singapore.

Singapore pushes first international benchmark for generative AI testing

results.title: SEA Ltd. Expands to U.S., Launches Migoo AI Chatbot to Boost Growth & Profit :: AktienSensor
results.text:
# SEA Ltd. Expands to U.S., Launches Migoo AI Chatbot to Boost Growth & Profit :: AktienSensor

Published: 2026-06-19T06:37:28+02:00
Source: aktiensensor.com (aktiensensor.com)
Language: en

## Story

SEA Ltd. Expands to U.S., Launches Migoo AI Chatbot to Boost Growth & Profit :: AktienSensor

# SEA Ltd. Expands to U.S., Launches Migoo AI Chatbot to Boost Growth & Profit

https://aktiensensor.com/articles/sea-ltd.-expands-to-u.s.-launches-migoo-ai-chatbot-to-boost-growth--profit/

## German Stocks Weekly Briefing

The most important German stocks & market movements of the week summarized.

- Current market trends & price movements
- Top analyses of German companies

Request for free now

Note: I am subscribing to the free newsletter. You can unsubscribe anytime. Terms and Conditions

## Corporate News Report

### Overview of Recent Developments at SEA Ltd. (ADR)

SEA Ltd. (ADR) announced a series of strategic initiatives aimed at reinforcing its market position in the United States and leveraging artificial intelligence to improve customer engagement. The company’s shares rose modestly following the disclosure, a movement that signals a degree of investor confidence in the company’s expansion plans.

### Market Context and Investor Reaction

The modest upward trajectory of SEA’s share price reflects the market’s perception that the company’s U.S. expansion and the launch of the Migoo chatbot represent tangible drivers of future earnings. While the price movement is relatively small, it indicates that investors view these initiatives as credible and potentially valuable for long‑term value creation.

### Strategic Initiatives

| Initiative | Objective | Expected Impact |
| --- | --- | --- |
| U.S. Expansion | Broaden distribution channels and market share in North America | Increased revenue streams, diversification of geographic risk |
| Migoo AI Chatbot | Enhance customer support, reduce operational costs | Improved customer satisfaction, lower service costs, data capture for product development |

These initiatives align with broader industry trends that emphasize digital transformation and geographic diversification as key levers for growth.

### Sector Analysis and Competitive Positioning

#### 1. Technology‑Driven Customer Engagement

The deployment of AI‑powered chatbots has become a standard practice in sectors ranging from retail to telecommunications. Companies that integrate natural‑language processing and machine learning into customer service can achieve higher engagement rates while simultaneously reducing per‑interaction costs. SEA’s Migoo positions the company alongside industry leaders such as Salesforce, Zendesk, and IBM, who have successfully deployed similar solutions. The success of Migoo will depend on the quality of its conversational AI, the breadth of its integration across SEA’s product portfolio, and its ability to generate actionable insights for marketing and product development.

#### 2. Geographic Diversification

Expanding into the U.S. market offers SEA access to one of the largest consumer bases globally and mitigates reliance on domestic markets, which may be subject to regulatory changes or economic downturns. This strategy mirrors the diversification paths taken by firms such as Atlassian, Shopify, and Slack, which have achieved substantial growth by establishing a strong presence in the United States. Geographic diversification also provides opportunities to cross‑sell complementary products and capture synergies in marketing and logistics.

#### 3. Financial and Operational Metrics

Analysts will examine SEA’s revenue growth rates, gross margin stability, and customer acquisition costs to gauge the effectiveness of its expansion and digital initiatives. A sustained increase in average revenue per user (ARPU) and a narrowing of customer support costs post‑Migoo adoption would signal a positive return on investment.

### Economic Drivers Beyond the Industry

Digital Adoption Pace The accelerated shift toward digital platforms, driven by the COVID‑19 pandemic, has increased demand for AI‑enabled services. Companies that can swiftly adapt to evolving customer expectations will likely capture market share from less agile competitors.

Data Monetization AI chatbots generate vast amounts of structured customer data. When leveraged responsibly, this data can inform product development, targeted marketing, and predictive maintenance, thereby creating additional revenue streams.

Cost Pressures and Efficiency Rising labor costs, especially in the U.S. market, amplify the value proposition of automation. By reducing dependence on human agents, SEA can improve margins and better manage operational risk.

Regulatory Environment Data privacy regulations (e.g., GDPR, CCPA) influence how AI chatbots collect and process customer information. SEA’s compliance strategy will be critical to avoid legal penalties and maintain consumer trust.

### Cross‑Sector Insights

- Retail & E‑Commerce: Similar to how Amazon utilizes AI for customer interactions, SEA’s Migoo can improve upsell opportunities by providing personalized recommendations during chat sessions.
- Financial Services: Banks that use AI chatbots to handle routine inquiries have reported significant reductions in call center volume. SEA could adopt comparable cost‑saving models.
- Healthcare: AI chatbots in healthcare settings enhance patient engagement and streamline triage processes. The success in this sector underscores the importance of user experience and trust, lessons SEA can apply in designing Migoo.

### Conclusion

SEA Ltd. (ADR) demonstrates a clear strategic direction by focusing on U.S. expansion and the adoption of AI technology through Migoo. These moves align with prevailing economic trends toward digital transformation and geographic diversification. While the share price reaction has been modest, the market’s confidence suggests that investors are anticipating substantive benefits from these initiatives. Continued scrutiny of financial performance, customer satisfaction metrics, and competitive positioning will be essential for assessing the long‑term impact of SEA’s corporate strategy.
